mycielski_7.col.notes: Mycielski construction guarantees chi(M_k)=k=7, triangle-free (clique=2). Hard because no triangle structure for heuristics.

hard_3colorable_450.col.notes: Tripartite construction guarantees chi=3. Hard because dense inter-partition edges (p=0.35) obscure partition structure.

regular_15_500.col.notes: DSATUR upper bound=8, clique lower bound=4. True optimum unknown. Uniform degree=15, no exploitable structure.

kneser_9_3.col.notes: Lovász theorem: chi(KG(n,k))=n-2k+2=9-6+2=5. Triangle-free (clique=2), high chi relative to density.

hard_8color_300.col.notes: Partition construction guarantees chi=8 (no intra-group edges). Dense inter-group p=0.7 makes partition hard to find.

queen_11x11.col.notes: Classic DIMACS benchmark. Lower bound=11 (diagonal clique). DSATUR gives 15. True optimum likely 11.

dense_random_150_072.col.notes: DSATUR upper bound=39. Very dense (p=0.72), true optimum unknown. SA needs many restarts to escape local optima.

petersen_40_6.col.notes: Generalized Petersen GP(40,6) is 3-colorable. Regular cubic graph, hard to find 3-coloring despite low chi.

hard_6partite_360.col.notes: 6-partite construction guarantees chi=6. 20% random edge removal creates noise that hides partition structure.

regular_20_300.col.notes: DSATUR upper bound=9, 20-regular graph n=300. Uniform structure, flat landscape for both algorithms.
